message:
refactor(core+abi): collapse format_passwd/shadow_line into field structs + cargo fmt sweep

Two semantic changes, bundled with the incidental fmt reflows they pulled in:

1. `frankenlibc_core::pwd::format_passwd_line` and
   `frankenlibc_core::pwd::shadow::format_shadow_line` previously took 7 and
   9 positional arguments respectively. That shape invited callers to mix
   up adjacent integer fields (e.g. uid/gid, min/max/warn/inact) and made
   the putpwent/putspent ABI glue in unistd_abi.rs and
   glibc_internal_abi.rs difficult to read. Introduce named
   borrowed-field structs:

     pub struct PasswdLineFields<'a> {
         pub name, passwd, gecos, dir, shell: &'a [u8],
         pub uid, gid: u32,
     }

     pub struct ShadowLineFields<'a> {
         pub name, passwd: &'a [u8],
         pub lstchg, min, max, warn, inact, expire: i64,
         pub flag: u64,
     }

   Both helpers now take one argument plus the output buffer. All three
   call sites (the ABI wrappers in glibc_internal_abi.rs and
   unistd_abi.rs::putspent, plus the core unit tests in pwd/mod.rs) are
   updated, and the `#[derive(Copy)]` on each struct keeps the call sites
   allocation-free.

2. Minor correctness-hygiene cleanups that rustc/clippy started flagging
   once rustfmt ran:

   - `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs`:
     `AbiFs::dev_id` returned `self.0.st_dev as u64` — st_dev already is
     u64 on linux-gnu glibc, so the cast is a no-op / lint. Dropped.
     `addmntent`'s `fields.freq/passno` no longer need `as i32`: the
     libc mntent fields are already i32.
   - `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/lib.rs`: reorder `pub mod rpc;` after
     `pub mod resource;` so the module list is alphabetical again.
   - `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`: the "Parse a single
     line from /etc/protocols" block was a `///` doc comment attached
     to nothing (the item has been moved to `frankenlibc_core::resolv`).
     Demote it to `//` so rustdoc stops attaching it to the next item.

3. `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ftw/walker.rs` test module: extract the
   `(MockStat, Vec<Vec<u8>>, Option<Vec<u8>>)` tuple into `MockNode` and
   the BTreeMap key into `MockNodes` — silences `clippy::type_complexity`
   in the mock fs harness without touching walker behavior.

Everything else in this commit is `cargo fmt` reflow — we were running
with a drifted fmt profile from a stale toolchain and pulling formatting
out of the refactor commits made both harder to review. The reflow
touches err_abi, glibc_internal_abi, pwd_abi, resolv_abi, unistd_abi in
the abi crate, and err, ether, ftw, getopt (parse+state), grp, mntent,
netgroup, resolv, rpc in the core crate. No behavior changes from the
reflow — cargo check + tests remain green.

Excludes stdio_abi + stdio_abi_test which carry a separate fmemopen
behavior fix (next commit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
